predictions.question: Return the first name, last name and email of the owners living in a state whose name contains the substring 'North'.?

predictions.question: What are the first names of all players, and their average rankings?
predictions.generated_sql: SELECT first_name, AVG(ranking) AS avg_ranking FROM players GROUP BY first_name
predictions.reference_sql: SELECT AVG(ranking) , T1.first_name FROM players AS T1 JOIN rankings AS T2 ON T1.player_id = T2.player_id GROUP BY T1.first_name

predictions.question: How many pets are owned by students that have an age greater than 20?
predictions.generated_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT petid) FROM has_pet JOIN student ON has_pet.stuid = student.stuid WHERE student.age > 20
predictions.reference_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM student AS T1 JOIN has_pet AS T2 ON T1.stuid = T2.stuid WHERE T1.age > 20

predictions.question: What is the episode for the TV series named "Sky Radio"?

predictions.question: Who is enrolled in a Bachelor degree program? List the first name, middle name, last name.?
predictions.generated_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.first_name, T1.middle_name, T1.last_name FROM students AS T1 JOIN student_enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN degree_programs AS T3 ON T2.degree_program_id = T3.degree_program_id WHERE T3.degree_summary_name = 'Bachelor
predictions.reference_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.first_name , T1.middle_name , T1.last_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Degree_Programs AS T3 ON T2.degree_program_id = T3.degree_program_id WHERE T3.degree_summary_name = 'Bachelor'

predictions.question: Which model saves the most gasoline? That is to say, have the maximum miles per gallon.?
predictions.generated_sql: SELECT Model FROM CARS_DATA WHERE Cylinders = (SELECT MAX(Cylinders) FROM CARS_DATA)
predictions.reference_sql: SELECT T1.Model FROM CAR_NAMES AS T1 JOIN CARS_DATA AS T2 ON T1.MakeId = T2.Id ORDER BY T2.mpg DESC LIMIT 1

predictions.question: What are the id and names of the countries which have more than 3 car makers or produce the 'fiat' model?
predictions.generated_sql: SELECT c.id, c.countryname FROM countries c JOIN car_makers cm ON c.id = cm.countryid GROUP BY c.id, c.countryname HAVING COUNT(cm.id) > 3 UNION SELECT c.id, c.countryname FROM countries c JOIN model_list m ON c.id = m.countryid WHERE m.model = 'fiat
predictions.reference_sql: SELECT T1.countryId , T1.CountryName FROM Countries AS T1 JOIN CAR_MAKERS AS T2 ON T1.CountryId = T2.Country GROUP BY T1.countryId HAVING COUNT(*) > 3 UNION SELECT T1.countryId , T1.CountryName FROM Countries AS T1 JOIN CAR_MAKERS AS T2 ON T1.CountryId = T2.Country JOIN MODEL_LIST AS T3 ON T2.Id = T3.Maker WHERE T3.Model = 'fiat'

predictions.question: What is the semester which most student registered in? Show both the name and the id.?
predictions.generated_sql: SELECT Semesters.semester_name, Semesters.semester_id FROM Semesters JOIN Student_Enrolment ON Semesters.semester_id = Student_Enrolment.semester_id GROUP BY Semesters.semester_id ORDER BY COUNT(Student_Enrolment.student_id) DESC LIMIT 1
predictions.reference_sql: SELECT T1.semester_name , T1.semester_id FROM Semesters AS T1 JOIN Student_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.semester_id = T2.semester_id GROUP BY T1.semester_id ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1

predictions.question: Who is the first student to register? List the first name, middle name and last name.?
predictions.generated_sql: SELECT first_name, middle_name, last_name FROM students ORDER BY student_id LIMIT 1
predictions.reference_sql: SELECT first_name , middle_name , last_name FROM Students ORDER BY date_first_registered ASC LIMIT 1

predictions.question: Return the template type description of the template type with the code AD.?
predictions.generated_sql: SELECT template_type_description FROM Ref_template_types WHERE template_type_code = 'AD.

predictions.question: Return the number of flights.?
predictions.generated_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM FLIGHTS
predictions.reference_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM FLIGHTS

predictions.question: What are the names of the dogs for which the owner has not spend more than 1000 for treatment ?
predictions.generated_sql: SELECT name FROM Dogs WHERE owner_id IN (SELECT owner_id FROM Charges WHERE charge_amount <= 1000)
predictions.reference_sql: SELECT name FROM dogs WHERE dog_id NOT IN ( SELECT dog_id FROM treatments GROUP BY dog_id HAVING SUM(cost_of_treatment) > 1000 )

predictions.question: Find the average age of students who do not have any pet .?
predictions.generated_sql: SELECT AVG(age) FROM student WHERE stuid NOT IN (SELECT stuid FROM has_pet)
predictions.reference_sql: SELECT AVG(age) FROM student WHERE stuid NOT IN (SELECT stuid FROM has_pet)

predictions.question: How many flights do we have?
predictions.generated_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM FLIGHTS

predictions.question: What is the maximum and minimum share for the TV series?
predictions.generated_sql: SELECT MAX(SHARE) AS max_share, MIN(SHARE) AS min_share FROM TV_series
predictions.reference_sql: SELECT MAX(SHARE) , MIN(SHARE) FROM TV_series

predictions.question: How many templates do we have?

predictions.question: Find all airlines that have fewer than 200 flights.?
predictions.generated_sql: SELECT Airline FROM FLIGHTS GROUP BY Airline HAVING COUNT(FlightNo) < 200
predictions.reference_sql: SELECT T1.Airline FROM AIRLINES AS T1 JOIN FLIGHTS AS T2 ON T1.uid = T2.Airline GROUP BY T1.Airline HAVING COUNT(*) < 200

predictions.question: Find the name of the employee who got the highest one time bonus.?
predictions.generated_sql: SELECT name FROM employee WHERE bonus = (SELECT MAX(bonus) FROM employee WHERE bonus IS NOT NULL AND bonus != 0)
predictions.reference_sql: SELECT t1.name FROM employee AS t1 JOIN evaluation AS t2 ON t1.Employee_ID = t2.Employee_ID ORDER BY t2.bonus DESC LIMIT 1

predictions.question: What are the id, name and membership level of visitors who have spent the largest amount of money in total in all museum tickets?
predictions.generated_sql: SELECT v.id, v.name, m.Level_of_membership FROM visitor v JOIN visit vi ON v.id = vi.visitor_id JOIN museum m ON vi.museum_id = m.Museum_ID ORDER BY vi.Total_spent DESC LIMIT 1
predictions.reference_sql: SELECT t2.visitor_id , t1.name , t1.Level_of_membership FROM visitor AS t1 JOIN visit AS t2 ON t1.id = t2.visitor_id GROUP BY t2.visitor_id ORDER BY SUM(t2.Total_spent) DESC LIMIT 1

predictions.question: Show the ids of high schoolers who have friends and are also liked by someone else.?
predictions.generated_sql: SELECT H1.id FROM Highschooler H1 JOIN Friend F1 ON H1.id = F1.id JOIN Friend F2 ON H1.id = F2.student_id JOIN Highschooler H2 ON F2.id = H2.id JOIN Likes L ON H2.id = L.student_id
predictions.reference_sql: SELECT student_id FROM Friend INTERSECT SELECT liked_id FROM Likes

predictions.question: Return the money rank of the poker player with the greatest height.?
predictions.generated_sql: SELECT MAX(Money_Rank) FROM people WHERE Height = ( SELECT MAX(Height ) FROM people )
predictions.reference_sql: SELECT T2.Money_Rank FROM people AS T1 JOIN poker_player AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID ORDER BY T1.Height DESC LIMIT 1

predictions.question: Find the program which most number of students are enrolled in. List both the id and the summary.?
predictions.generated_sql: SELECT degree_program_id, degree_summary_name FROM degree_programs WHERE degree_program_id = (SELECT degree_program_id FROM students GROUP BY degree_program_id ORDER BY COUNT(degree_program_id) DESC LIMIT 1)
predictions.reference_sql: SELECT T1.degree_program_id , T1.degree_summary_name FROM Degree_Programs AS T1 JOIN Student_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.degree_program_id = T2.degree_program_id GROUP BY T1.degree_program_id ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1

predictions.question: Give the nationality that is most common across all people.?
predictions.generated_sql: SELECT Nationality FROM people GROUP BY Nationality ORDER BY COUNT(Nationality) DESC LIMIT 1
predictions.reference_sql: SELECT Nationality FROM people GROUP BY Nationality ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1

predictions.question: Find the average life expectancy and total population for each continent where the average life expectancy is shorter than 72?
predictions.generated_sql: SELECT AVG(LifeExpectancy), SUM(Population) FROM world_1.country GROUP BY Continent HAVING AVG(LifeExpectancy) < 72
predictions.reference_sql: SELECT SUM(Population) , AVG(LifeExpectancy) , Continent FROM country GROUP BY Continent HAVING AVG(LifeExpectancy) < 72

predictions.question: What are the names and release years for all the songs of the youngest singer?
predictions.generated_sql: SELECT T2.song_name, T2.song_release_year FROM singer AS T1 JOIN singer_in_concert AS T2 ON T1.name = T2.singer_name JOIN concert AS T3 ON T2.concert_name = T3.concert_name JOIN ( SELECT name, MIN(age) AS min_age FROM singer GROUP BY name ) AS T4 ON T1.name = T4.name WHERE T1.age = T4.min_age
predictions.reference_sql: SELECT song_name , song_release_year FROM singer ORDER BY age LIMIT 1

predictions.question: List the Episode of all TV series showed on TV Channel with series name "Sky Radio".?
predictions.generated_sql: SELECT Episode FROM TV_series WHERE series_name = 'Sky Radio

predictions.question: Find the kind of program which most number of students are enrolled in?
predictions.generated_sql: SELECT degree_summary_name FROM degree_programs WHERE degree_program_id = (SELECT degree_program_id FROM students GROUP BY degree_program_id ORDER BY COUNT(degree_program_id) DESC LIMIT 1)
predictions.reference_sql: SELECT T1.degree_summary_name FROM Degree_Programs AS T1 JOIN Student_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.degree_program_id = T2.degree_program_id GROUP BY T1.degree_summary_name ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1

predictions.question: How many degrees does the engineering department have?
predictions.generated_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT degree_program_id) FROM degree_programs JOIN departments ON degree_programs.department_id = departments.department_id WHERE department_name = 'Engineering
